steps.title: Read circle A
steps.content: $x^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 36$ is in the form $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$, so circle A has center $(0, 2)$ and radius $6$.

steps.title: Shift the center down 4 units
steps.content: Shifting down decreases $y$ by 4, so the new center is $(0, 2 - 4) = (0, -2)$. The radius is unchanged: still $6$, so $r^2 = 36$. Circle B: $(x - 0)^2 + (y - (-2))^2 = 36$, i.e. $x^2 + (y + 2)^2 = 36$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• $x^2 + (y + 2)^2 = 36$ — center $(0, -2)$, radius $6$. Matches.
  • $x^2 + (y - 6)^2 = 36$ — center $(0, 6)$; that would be a shift up by 4, not down.
  • $(x - 4)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 36$ — center $(4, 2)$; this is a horizontal shift, not vertical.
  • $(x + 4)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 36$ — center $(-4, 2)$; again a horizontal shift.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
